themes.exampleQuote: A perfect speakeasy vibe with the best cocktails I've had in Pittsburgh.
A superb riff on a Sazerac/Manhattan/old fashioned. Slightly sweet, little liquorice flavor, booze heavy.
Alicia was one of the best bartenders I've had, definitely deserves the bar manager position.
We were informed we had to leave because there is a 90-minute time limit, only mentioned in the menu in very small print.
I have tried to get in a few times but it is always super packed.

notable_quote: I will be making the drive from Cleveland just for this bar in the near future.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers keep coming back to the moody candlelit room, the bartender-driven cocktail creativity, and specific staff by name; the friction point is a 90-minute seating policy that catches guests off guard at the moment they order another round.
quarter_narrative: Commerce Bar spent Spring 2026 as one of East Liberty's most talked-about rooms, pulling visitors from Cleveland and earning repeat-visit pledges in nearly every review. The 83 percent five-star rate on 106 Google reviews is a strong floor, but the Instagram account posted once in 30 days and produced zero reels, while no TikTok creator has indexed the bar at all. That silence is notable for a place reviewers call an 'entire experience' with a 'moody, vintage, and botanical' aesthetic, exactly the kind of environment that travels well on video. Hidden Harbor leads the Pittsburgh Bars family this issue on social momentum, a signal Commerce Bar has the raw material to compete on but hasn't yet converted. Closing the gap between the room's word-of-mouth pull and its near-zero social footprint is the clearest path up the family rankings.
tldr_read: Strong review sentiment and a room people drive from Cleveland for, paired with a near-dormant Instagram and zero TikTok presence. Neighborhood Staples, ranked second in Pittsburgh Bars.
tldr_meaning: The review floor is solid, but the social signals are too quiet for a bar with this level of in-person word-of-mouth, and the gap to the family leader runs almost entirely through momentum and visual catalog.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Second In Pittsburgh Bars On Word Of Mouth Alone, No Social Signal To Show For It.

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram with weekly reels
playbook.action: One reel per week of the room at golden hour or a cocktail build; the account posted once in 30 days and has zero reels on record.

playbook.headline: Claim a website and phone on Google
playbook.action: Commerce Bar has no website and no phone number on its Google profile, both fields are blank while the conversion path sits well below the family median.

playbook.headline: Tag the seating policy before the check lands
playbook.action: The single negative review stems from the 90-minute limit surfacing at checkout; a verbal heads-up at the second round would neutralize the recurring complaint before it repeats.
